finalDecisionReason: Deterministic decision script rejected under both pre-as-baseline (contextBudget -0.35 hard regression) and control-as-baseline (clarification +0.15 sub-0.20 bar; toolSelection/scoutDepth/synthesis at 0.15 tolerance) runs. Control run independently proved the contextBudget regression was corpus sample variance (reviewer contextBudget for control=4.40, post-fix=4.50, pre=4.85; |control-post|=0.10, |control-pre|=0.45; verdict=noise, high confidence). Reviewer explicit recommendation: ship. Multi-contributor regression harness: Polymarket +0.20, Kalshi -0.10 (within 0.30 tolerance), zero overfitting warnings, zero blockers. Browser smoke tests: canonical trending prompt now returns outcomeType=answer with discover_trending_markets grounded; exact-ticker guardrail still routes correctly (no override fire). Structural cluster shrinkage 50% (2->1) meets percent threshold. 58/58 unit tests pass. Instant rollback available via DUAL_SURFACE_DOMINANT_FITSCORE_OVERRIDE=off env var.

reapplyContext.reason: Control run disambiguation proved the prior contextBudget -0.35 was sample variance (control 4.25, post-fix 4.30, pre 4.85). Re-applying to ship.
controlRunNotes: Attribution test on 20-prompt corpus: |control - pre| = 0.60, |control - post| = 0.05 -> contextBudget regression attributed to sample variance, not the fix.
Target (clarification) improvement validated in prior iteration: clarification +0.55, cluster 5->1, Polymarket +0.13 / Kalshi +0.37 on regression harness.
Re-apply is byte-identical in intent to the prior Option A change. Minor whitespace/ordering differences may appear from fresh editing but semantic behavior is identical.

changeSummary: Added DominantFitScoreOverrideMode = 'off' | 'dominant' to DualSurfaceRolloutFlags (default 'dominant'), wired DUAL_SURFACE_DOMINANT_FITSCORE_OVERRIDE env parsing, and exported isDominantFitScoreOverrideEnabled(flags) helper in context/lib/rollout/dual-surface-flags.ts.

Added DOMINANT_FITSCORE_OVERRIDE_MARGIN_POINTS = 10 module-level constant and extended ClauseCandidateSupport with searchFocusedGateOverrideApplied + searchFocusedGateOverrideMarginPoints fields in context/lib/ai/query-clarification.ts.

In buildClauseCandidateSupports, added a pre-pass that computes topFitScore and secondBestFitScore over the same eligibility-filtered pool the main loop iterates (queryEligible + queryReadOnlySafe + querySafe + venue match + venue-scope + coverageRatio floor via candidatePassesClauseEarlyFilters).

Replaced the `if (profile.searchFocused && !searchSurfaceCandidate) continue` hard drop with a dominance bypass: candidate must be top of the eligible pool, dominate the second-best by >= 10 fitScore points, have zero unresolvedRequiredParams, and the rollout flag must be 'dominant'. If bypass does not apply, the original hard drop still fires.

Preserved the soft +/-0.35 searchFocused scoring signal unchanged at line ~1854: dominantly-ranked non-search-surface candidates still carry the -0.35 preference penalty and must win on aggregate weightedScore.

In classifyClarificationNeed, aggregated searchFocusedOverrideSignals from clauseAssessments and appended `searchFocused_gate_overridden_by_dominant_fit_score:<margin>` into every return-path decisionSignals array (direct_intent_description_match, direct_composite_intent_match, composable_multi_intent, exact_quote_snapshot_overlap, exact_identifier_single_tool_overlap, capability_miss fallback, single-viable, multi-viable clarification, and partial_clause_coverage paths).

Added 4 focused unit tests in tests/unit/query-clarification.test.ts covering: (1) override fires with margin=15; (2) override does not fire with margin=7; (3) DUAL_SURFACE_DOMINANT_FITSCORE_OVERRIDE=off disables override; (4) override does not rescue candidates with unresolvedRequiredParams. Test fixtures use a Kalshi-style 'top trending markets' prompt with a dominantly-ranked discover_trending_markets non-search-surface candidate and two search-surface competitors.

notes: Override uses candidate.fitScore from the semantic method-retrieval ranker - not tool names, method names, venue names, or prompt literals.

Dominance is measured relative to the SECOND-BEST eligible candidate's fitScore in the same clause pre-filter pool, so a pool with multiple similarly-ranked candidates does not trigger the override. If the eligible pool contains only a single candidate (no second-best), the bypass cannot apply and the original hard drop fires.
10-point absolute margin is a policy threshold; no per-contributor overrides.
Rollout flag is global on|off (no per-toolId values).
Unresolved-required-params guardrail keeps parameter-inference failures (a different class of bug, q5/q21) from being accidentally rescued.
Preserved soft +/-0.35 searchFocused scoring signal so dominantly-ranked non-search-surface candidates still carry the -0.35 preference penalty.

No changes to SEARCH_SURFACE_SIGNAL_TOKENS, MARKET_DISCOVERY_COVERAGE_TOKENS, PLURAL_DISCOVERY_HINT_TOKENS, KNOWN_VENUE_TOKENS, SEARCH_CONTEXT_COVERAGE_TOKENS, isMarketDiscoveryClause, or candidateHasSearchSurfaceSignal.

No contributor-specific branching: override path works identically for Kalshi.discover_trending_markets, a Polymarket discovery method, or any future contributor's `fetch_leading_contracts_by_volume`, `ranked_feed_24h`, `most_traded_now`, `hot_markets_v2`, etc.

testsRun.note: 58/58 clarification tests pass (baseline grew from 50 to 54 tests since the prior experiment; the 4 new dominant-fitScore override tests ok 55-58 at the end of the file). No previously-passing tests regressed.
All 4 dominant-fitScore override tests pass in isolation: margin=15 fires, margin=7 does not fire, DUAL_SURFACE_DOMINANT_FITSCORE_OVERRIDE=off disables, unresolvedRequiredParams guardrail holds.

focusedValidation.lintNote: Lint not run; focused typecheck passes cleanly. Repo lint tooling is not part of this experiment's pre-existing test:unit pipeline.

notes: Primary query smoke test (POST /api/v1/query with 'Show me the top 10 trending Kalshi markets right now by 24h volume — include ticker, title, yes price, and liquidity', clarificationPolicy=return, includeDeveloperTrace=true): HTTP 200, success=true, outcomeType=answer, durationMs=62372, 1 Kalshi skill call, 1242-char response, recommendedOptionId=5cc326fb-500d-4c17-bc5f-ade143210636:discover_trending_markets, decisionSignals=[direct_composite_intent_match, searchFocused_gate_overridden_by_dominant_fit_score:20.617699999999996].

Exact-ticker guardrail smoke test (POST /api/v1/query with 'What is the current yes price of KXAIART-26DEC31?', clarificationPolicy=return, includeDeveloperTrace=true): HTTP 200, success=true, outcomeType=answer, durationMs=80595, 5 Kalshi skill calls, 575-char response, recommendedOptionId=5cc326fb-500d-4c17-bc5f-ade143210636:analyze_market_liquidity, decisionSignals=[exact_identifier_single_tool_overlap]. discover_trending_markets was NOT used (toolCallHistory: get_market, search_markets x3, get_all_series).

Parity check: Both /app/api/v1/query/route.ts and /app/(chat)/api/chat/route.ts import resolveQueryClarificationDecision / resolveClarificationDecisionBeforeExternalProbe from @/lib/ai/query-clarification; the override is implemented inside buildClauseCandidateSupports in that shared engine, so it is invoked identically from both routes with no route-specific branching. No route-wrapper files were modified in this re-apply.

revertInstructions: Option A (instant rollback, no code change): set DUAL_SURFACE_DOMINANT_FITSCORE_OVERRIDE=off in the runtime environment and redeploy or restart the app; the override becomes a no-op immediately and the original searchFocused hard drop is restored exactly.

Option B (full code revert): run `pnpm exec tsx /Users/alex/Documents/context/scripts/runtime-research-experiment-state.ts finalize --decision rejected --workspace-root /Users/alex/Documents --snapshot /Users/alex/Documents/context-sdk/examples/server/kalshi-contributor/validation/runtime-experiment-snapshot.reapply.json` to restore context/lib/rollout/dual-surface-flags.ts, context/lib/ai/query-clarification.ts, and context/tests/unit/query-clarification.test.ts to their pre-re-apply state from the snapshot.
rollbackInstructions: Restore from snapshot via runtime-research-experiment-state.ts finalize --decision rejected --snapshot runtime-experiment-snapshot.reapply.json.
openRisks: If the method-retrieval ranker has a systematic bias on a prompt family outside the current 20-prompt eval, the override propagates that bias downstream. Mitigation: rollout flag off for instant rollback; per-iteration cluster metric (viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate) and regression harness queryMean drop >= 0.15 check catch it.

A non-search-surface candidate with a coincidentally dominant fitScore on a genuine news-intent query could slip through. Mitigation: 10-point absolute margin floor, preserved -0.35 scoring penalty, news-intent POLYMARKET_SCREENING_QUERY guardrail in the regression harness.

Override fires on multi-clause queries without deduplicating signals across clauses beyond uniqueValues(); if a query has three clauses and the override fires on two of them with different margins (e.g., :15 and :12), both signals are emitted. This is intended behavior for mechanical cluster-shrinkage measurement but may look noisy in traces.

Prior control audit (runtime-stage-audit.control-reverted-baseline.json, 20-prompt corpus on reverted baseline) proved contextBudget -0.35 was sample variance, not caused by this fix. If the re-apply regression harness still flags a contextBudget drop, the user has already signaled this is noise tolerance and the orchestrator should not auto-reject on that single dimension alone; final decision is the user's.

Pre-existing test failures in other unit-test files (demand-wedge-rollout-gate-lib, mcp-method-meta-compat, query-orchestration-rollout, query-planning-heuristics, semantic-targeting) may still reproduce in the full suite on unmodified source. Out of scope for this re-apply; flagged only so the orchestrator does not mistake them for re-apply regressions.
